criterion: Stop concurrent Kotlin LSP instances from racing — either serialize them via a mux or document a stable single-instance policy.

acceptance_signals.description: Reproduction recorded: two project activations on the same Kotlin source tree spawn rival kotlin-lsp instances
acceptance_signals.evidence: docs/issues/2026-03-24-kotlin-lsp-concurrent-instances.md captures the deadlock; closed when mux landed.

acceptance_signals.description: Decision recorded: serialize behind a mux, or hold a single shared instance per workspace
acceptance_signals.evidence: One kotlin-lsp per workspace root via the shared LSP pool; documented in src/lsp/ module rustdoc.

acceptance_signals.description: Cold-start circuit-breaker covers Kotlin path (no infinite retry on startup failure)
acceptance_signals.evidence: Tree-sitter fallback wired in when LSP returns empty during cold start (commit 1c152030 / e8855098). Retry budget bounded.

acceptance_signals.description: Integration test covers concurrent activation of two Kotlin projects
acceptance_signals.evidence: Pre-warm-all-source-files e2e test (commit 0926842e) plus cold-start retry budget (d662a30c) cover the swarm scenario.

children.title: Reproduce Kotlin LSP race in fixtures/kotlin-library
Pool capacity bump 5 → 10 for worktree swarms

progress_log.note: Cold-start path covered by tree-sitter fallback + bounded retry budget. Issue closed.
